caption: HTTP Request
description: The HTTP Request object represents the attributes of a request made to a web server. It encapsulates the details and metadata associated with an HTTP request, including the request method, headers, URL, query parameters, body content, and other relevant information.
name: http_request

body_length.caption: Request Body Length
body_length.description: The actual length of the HTTP request body, in number of bytes, independent of a potentially existing Content-Length header.

http_method.caption: HTTP Method
http_method.description: The HTTP request method indicates the desired action to be performed for a given resource.

CONNECT.caption: Connect
CONNECT.description: The CONNECT method establishes a tunnel to the server identified by the target resource.

DELETE.caption: Delete
DELETE.description: The DELETE method deletes the specified resource.

GET.caption: Get
GET.description: The GET method requests a representation of the specified resource. Requests using GET should only retrieve data.

HEAD.caption: Head
HEAD.description: The HEAD method asks for a response identical to a GET request, but without the response body.

OPTIONS.caption: Options
OPTIONS.description: The OPTIONS method describes the communication options for the target resource.

PATCH.caption: Patch
PATCH.description: The PATCH method applies partial modifications to a resource.

POST.caption: Post
POST.description: The POST method submits an entity to the specified resource, often causing a change in state or side effects on the server.

PUT.caption: Put
PUT.description: The PUT method replaces all current representations of the target resource with the request payload.

TRACE.caption: Trace
TRACE.description: The TRACE method performs a message loop-back test along the path to the target resource.

ACL.caption: ACL
ACL.description: The ACL method modifies the access control list of the specified resource.

BASELINE-CONTROL.caption: Baseline Control
BASELINE-CONTROL.description: The BASELINE-CONTROL method places the specified collection resource under baseline control.

BIND.caption: Bind
BIND.description: The BIND method adds a binding to the specified collection resource.

CHECKIN.caption: Check In
CHECKIN.description: The CHECKIN method creates a new version from a checked-out version-controlled resource.

CHECKOUT.caption: Check Out
CHECKOUT.description: The CHECKOUT method prepares a version-controlled resource for modification.

COPY.caption: Copy
COPY.description: The COPY method duplicates the specified resource.

LABEL.caption: Label
LABEL.description: The LABEL method adds, sets, or removes labels on a version-controlled resource.

LINK.caption: Link
LINK.description: The LINK method establishes one or more links between resources.

LOCK.caption: Lock
LOCK.description: The LOCK method acquires or refreshes a lock on the specified resource.

MERGE.caption: Merge
MERGE.description: The MERGE method merges changes from another resource into the specified resource.

MKACTIVITY.caption: Make Activity
MKACTIVITY.description: The MKACTIVITY method creates a new activity resource.

MKCALENDAR.caption: Make Calendar
MKCALENDAR.description: The MKCALENDAR method creates a new calendar collection resource.

MKCOL.caption: Make Collection
MKCOL.description: The MKCOL method creates a new collection resource.

MKREDIRECTREF.caption: Make Redirect Reference
MKREDIRECTREF.description: The MKREDIRECTREF method creates a new redirect reference resource.

MKWORKSPACE.caption: Make Workspace
MKWORKSPACE.description: The MKWORKSPACE method creates a new workspace resource.

MOVE.caption: Move
MOVE.description: The MOVE method atomically duplicates and deletes the specified resource.

ORDERPATCH.caption: Order Patch
ORDERPATCH.description: The ORDERPATCH method changes the ordering of members in an ordered collection.

PRI.caption: PRI
PRI.description: The PRI method appears in the HTTP/2 connection preface to confirm the protocol.

PROPFIND.caption: Property Find
PROPFIND.description: The PROPFIND method retrieves properties defined on the specified resource.

PROPPATCH.caption: Property Patch
PROPPATCH.description: The PROPPATCH method sets and/or removes properties defined on the specified resource.

QUERY.caption: Query
QUERY.description: The QUERY method performs a safe query request that can include a request body.

REBIND.caption: Rebind
REBIND.description: The REBIND method moves a binding from one collection to another.

REPORT.caption: Report
REPORT.description: The REPORT method retrieves a report about the specified resource.

SEARCH.caption: Search
SEARCH.description: The SEARCH method searches a resource or collection using the request payload.

UNBIND.caption: Unbind
UNBIND.description: The UNBIND method removes a binding from the specified collection resource.

UNCHECKOUT.caption: Un-Check Out
UNCHECKOUT.description: The UNCHECKOUT method cancels a checkout and restores the resource to its previous state.

UNLINK.caption: Unlink
UNLINK.description: The UNLINK method removes one or more links between resources.

UNLOCK.caption: Unlock
UNLOCK.description: The UNLOCK method releases a lock on the specified resource.

UPDATE.caption: Update
UPDATE.description: The UPDATE method updates a version-controlled resource to match a specified version.

UPDATEREDIRECTREF.caption: Update Redirect Reference
UPDATEREDIRECTREF.description: The UPDATEREDIRECTREF method changes the target of a redirect reference resource.

VERSION-CONTROL.caption: Version Control
VERSION-CONTROL.description: The VERSION-CONTROL method places the specified resource under version control.

length.caption: Request Length
length.description: The length of the entire HTTP request, in number of bytes.

uid.description: The unique identifier of the http request.

url.description: The URL object that pertains to the request.
